help.title: Configure Google Sheets Authentication
help.markdown:
#### Choosing an authentication type

- **Google JWT File**: provides access to private spreadsheets and works in all environments where Grafana is running.
- **API Key**: simpler configuration, but requires spreadsheets to be public.
- **GCE Default Service Account**: automatically retrieves default credentials. Requires Grafana to be running on a Google Compute Engine virtual machine.

Select an Authentication type below and expand **Configure Google Sheets Authentication** for detailed guidance on configuration.

fields.description: Legacy authentication type field. Older provisioning stored the auth type here; the backend copies its value into `authenticationType` on load. Prefer `authenticationType` for new configurations.

help.title: Generate an API key
help.markdown:
1. Open the [Google Sheets](https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/library/sheets.googleapis.com?q=sheet) page in the API Library and enable access for your account.
2. Open the [Credentials page](https://console.developers.google.com/apis/credentials) in the Google API Console.
3. Click **Create Credentials** and then click **API key**.
4. Copy the key and paste it in the API Key field below. The file contents are encrypted and saved in the Grafana database.

fields.label: Private key path
fields.description: Paste private key or provide path to private file

ui.placeholder: File location of your private key (e.g. /etc/secrets/gce.pem)

fields.label: Private key
fields.description: Paste private key or provide path to private file

Legacy write-only secret used by older versions of the plugin. The backend still copies its decrypted value into memory but no runtime code path depends on it; new configurations should use `privateKey` instead.

fields.label: Default Spreadsheet ID
fields.description: Optional spreadsheet ID to use as default when creating new queries

relationships.description: Alternative sources for the JWT private key: either supply the key inline as a secret (privateKey) or point at a file path on the Grafana server (privateKeyPath). Exactly one of the two must be configured for jwt auth; privateKeyPath takes precedence when both are set.

instructions.msg: Three authentication methods are available, selected via jsonData.authenticationType: 'jwt' (Google JWT File, the default and used for private spreadsheets), 'key' (API Key, only works with public spreadsheets), and 'gce' (GCE Default Service Account, only works when Grafana runs on a Google Compute Engine VM). Configure exactly one method; the backend switches on this discriminator and only the credentials of the selected method are used.

To configure API Key authentication, set jsonData.authenticationType to 'key' and put the key in secureJsonData.apiKey. Minimal payload: {"jsonData":{"authenticationType":"key"},"secureJsonData":{"apiKey":"<api key>"}}. API keys only grant access to spreadsheets that are shared publicly (anyone with the link can view). Enable the Google Sheets API for the project the key belongs to.

To configure Google JWT File authentication, set jsonData.authenticationType to 'jwt' and provide jsonData.defaultProject, jsonData.clientEmail, jsonData.tokenUri, and the service account's private key. The private key may be supplied inline as secureJsonData.privateKey OR by pointing jsonData.privateKeyPath at a file on the Grafana server (the file may be a raw PEM or a service-account JSON containing a private_key field). Do not set both — inline privateKey takes precedence only when privateKeyPath is empty. Minimal inline payload: {"jsonData":{"authenticationType":"jwt","defaultProject":"<project>","clientEmail":"<sa email>","tokenUri":"https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token"},"secureJsonData":{"privateKey":"-----BEGIN PRIVATE KEY-----
...
-----END PRIVATE KEY-----
"}}. Also enable both the Google Sheets and Google Drive APIs on the project; Drive is used to list spreadsheets the service account has access to.

To configure GCE Default Service Account authentication, set jsonData.authenticationType to 'gce' and (optionally) jsonData.defaultProject. Minimal payload: {"jsonData":{"authenticationType":"gce"}}. The backend obtains credentials from the GCE metadata server; this only works when Grafana runs on a Google Compute Engine VM whose service account has been granted access to the required spreadsheets and to the Sheets and Drive APIs.

Legacy auth interpretation: older provisioned datasources stored the auth type in jsonData.authType (not jsonData.authenticationType). The backend LoadSettings copies authType into authenticationType when authType is set (pkg/models/settings.go), so both keys refer to the same value. New configurations should write only jsonData.authenticationType. Old JWT configurations may also carry the credential blob in secureJsonData.jwt; the backend still decrypts it for backward compatibility but does not use it — supply the individual fields (clientEmail, tokenUri, defaultProject, privateKey) instead.

secureJsonData values (apiKey, privateKey, jwt) are write-only: they can be set or reset but never read back. To check whether a secret is already configured on an existing datasource, read the secureJsonFields object (e.g. secureJsonFields.apiKey == true) instead of secureJsonData.
